name: Robinson School
about: Robinson School is an independent, English-language, coeducational, college-preparatory day school in the Condado area of San Juan, Puerto Rico. Founded in 1902, it was the first English-speaking college-preparatory school on the island and serves students from toddler age through grade 12. It is San Juan's only International Baccalaureate (IB) World School, authorized for the Primary, Middle Years, and Diploma Programmes.
accreditation: Middle States Association (MSA-CESS); IB World School
financial_aid_notes: The school reports that roughly 40 percent of students receive some form of financial aid.

name: Portsmouth Abbey School
about: Portsmouth Abbey School is a Roman Catholic, Benedictine, coeducational boarding and day school for grades 9-12, located on a 525-acre campus along Narragansett Bay in Portsmouth, Rhode Island. Founded in 1926 by Father Hugh Diman, the school educates students in the Catholic intellectual tradition and is run in association with the monks of Portsmouth Abbey. It draws students from across the United States and many foreign nations.
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC)
notable_alumni: Sean Spicer, Christopher Buckley, William Ruckelshaus
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid grants are awarded based on demonstrated need, academic merit, and availability of funds.

name: St. Andrew's School
about: St. Andrew's School is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school in Barrington, Rhode Island, serving grades 6-12 plus a postgraduate year. Founded in 1893, it sits on an 80-acre campus and is known for individualized academic support alongside an International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme. The school enrolls a small student body with a low student-teacher ratio.
college_matriculation: Graduates have enrolled at institutions including Tufts University, New York University, Boston University, and the University of St Andrews (Scotland).
accreditation: New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC); IB World School
notable_alumni: Michael Carter-Williams, Bonzie Colson, Ricky Ledo, Cole Swider, Andrew Robinson
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid is awarded based on documented family need and availability of funds; a majority of students receive some aid.
athletic_conference: New England Preparatory School Athletic Council (NEPSAC)

name: St. George's School
about: St. George's School is an Episcopal, coeducational boarding and day school for grades 9-12 in Middletown, Rhode Island, set on a 125-acre hilltop campus overlooking the Atlantic Ocean near Newport. Founded in 1896 by the Episcopal minister John Byron Diman, the school offers a college-preparatory program with strong academics, athletics, and arts. About 80 percent of its students board.
accreditation: Association of Independent Schools of New England (AISNE); New England Association of Schools and Colleges (NEASC)
notable_alumni: Vincent Astor, Julie Bowen, Tucker Carlson, Howard Dean, Claiborne Pell, Prescott Bush
financial_aid_notes: About 33 percent of students receive financial aid, with an average grant of roughly $61,000.

name: Camden Military Academy
about: Camden Military Academy is a private, all-male military boarding school in Camden, South Carolina, serving grades 7-12 plus a postgraduate year. Recognized by the state as the official military academy of South Carolina, it enrolls roughly 270 cadets who all live on campus and participate in JROTC. The academy is known as one of the most affordable military boarding schools in the United States and offers dual-credit college courses through the University of South Carolina Lancaster.
financial_aid_notes: The academy offers financial assistance options and is marketed as one of the most affordable military boarding schools in the U.S.; 529 education savings plans may be used for tuition.

name: South Carolina Governor's School for the Arts and Humanities
about: The South Carolina Governor's School for the Arts and Humanities is a public, residential high school in Greenville, South Carolina, serving artistically talented sophomores, juniors, and seniors from across the state. Founded in 1999, it provides tuition-free pre-professional training in creative writing, dance, drama, film, music, and visual arts within a master-apprentice, arts-centered community. Students pay only residence hall and meal-plan fees, with financial assistance available so no student is denied entry for inability to pay.
accreditation: Accrediting Commission for Community and Precollegiate Arts Schools (ACCPAS)
notable_alumni: Danielle Brooks, Nicole Beharie, Patina Miller, Teyonah Parris
financial_aid_notes: Tuition is free; students pay residence hall and meal-plan fees. Need-based aid is administered through the Governor's School Foundation for South Carolina residents, and no student is denied entrance based on inability to pay.
